sourceMovie.title: Being John Malkovich
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Being John Malkovich works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pan's Labyrinth
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Being John Malkovich clicked for you, Pan's Labyrinth (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Mask
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Being John Malkovich clicked for you, The Mask (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Rocky Horror Picture Show
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Being John Malkovich clicked for you, The Rocky Horror Picture Show (1975) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Being John Malkovich?
faq.answer: Try Pan's Labyrinth, The Mask, The Rocky Horror Picture Show—each shares DNA with Being John Malkovich while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Being John Malkovich?
faq.answer: Being John Malkovich (1999) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Being John Malkovich worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
